texto: The Quiet WorldIn an effort to get people to lookinto each other’s eyes more,and also to appease the mutes,the government has decidedto allot each person exactly one hundred and sixty-seven words, per day.When the phone rings, I put it to my ear without saying hello. In the restaurant I point at chicken noodle soup.I am adjusting well to the new way.Late at night, I call my long distance lover, I only used fifty-nine today. When she doesn’t respond,I know she’s used up all her words, thirty-two and a third times.After that, we just sit on the line and listen to each other breathe.
explicacao: Citação de Jeffrey McDaniel sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The Quiet WorldIn an effort to get people to lookinto each other’s eyes more,and also to appease …” articula tempo, i-love-you, love em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: I mean, have you ever imaginedthe ocean is alive, and needs to tell us something important, and the only way it can talkis by making waves crash, and we just lounge there, drenched in cocoa butter, on towels with crappy novels and volleyballs, sipping spritzers, as the ocean uses all its strength to repeatthe same warning over and over?

texto: If you heard your lover scream in the next roomand you ran in and saw his pinkie on the floor, in a small puddle of blood.You wouldn't rush to the pinkie and say, 'Darling, are you OK? 'No, you'd wrap your arms around his shoulders and worry about the pinkie later.The same holds true if you heard the scream, ran in and saw his hand or -god forbid- his whole arm.But suppose you hear your lover scream in the next room, and you run in and his head is on the floor next to his body.Which do you rush to and comfort first?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jeffrey McDaniel que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. texto: We didn’t deny the obvious,but we didn’t entirely accept it either.I mean, we said hello to it each morningin the foyer. We patted its little headas it made a mess in the backyard,but we never nurtured it. Many nights the obvious showed upat our bedroom door, in its pajamas,unable to sleep, in need of a hug,and we just stared at it like an Armenian,or even worse— hid beneath the coversand pretended not to hear its tiny sobs.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jeffrey McDaniel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “We didn’t deny the obvious,but we didn’t entirely accept it either.I mean, we said hello to it ea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, poetry, obvious pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e boa-noite.

texto: When you were sleeping on the sofaI put my ear to your ear and listenedto the echo of your dreams.That is the ocean I want to dive in, merge with the bright fish, plankton and pirate ships.I walk up to people on the street that kind of look like youand ask them the questions I would ask you.Can we sit on a rooftop and watch stars dissolve into smokerising from a chimney? Can I swing like Tarzan in the jungle of your breathing? I don’t wish I was in your arms, I just wish I was peddling a bicycle toward your arms.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jeffrey McDaniel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados.
